personal.name: Amjad Rehawi
personal.tagline: Hardware engineer for things that move themselves.
personal.subTagline: Senior honors double major in Electrical and Mechanical Engineering at Santa Clara University. I build the hardware spine of machines that move on their own: high-voltage battery systems, motor control, embedded firmware, and autonomous aircraft.
personal.availability: Open to Summer 2027 internships and full-time roles for 2027

thesis.headline: The same stack that ships a 400-volt race car flies an autonomous aircraft.
thesis.body: Cells, busbars, BMS, motor drives, CAN, sensor chains, vehicle control. The work I do on FSAE tractive systems and traction inverters at STMicroelectronics is the same engineering that powers eVTOL propulsion, swarm coordination, and aerospace-grade power electronics. Raven is the proof: an 8-stage roadmap from a single tilt-rotor airframe to a 4-drone autonomous swarm.

pillars.title: Power electronics
pillars.desc: 420 V / 504 V packs, BMS, charging, precharge, IMD

pillars.title: Embedded firmware
pillars.desc: STM32, CAN, Lauterbach JTAG, full analog signal chains

pillars.title: Systems craft
pillars.desc: PCB safety review, thermal, mechanical integration

about.heading: One stack, two domains: electric vehicles and autonomous aircraft.
paragraphs: I'm a senior honors student at Santa Clara University, graduating in 2027 with a double major in Electrical and Mechanical Engineering and a 3.7 GPA. I learn by building the full stack, schematic to PCB to firmware to mechanical to control, and shipping the result to a hard deadline.

Since 2023 I've owned the high-voltage electrical stack on SCU's Formula SAE electric cars: programming the Orion BMS 2 on a ~420 V accumulator, designing and reviewing shutdown, precharge, and IMD safety circuits, architecting the vehicle CAN network, and soldering a 140+ thermistor harness whose placement strategy improved measurement accuracy by roughly 50 percent. At STMicroelectronics I debugged a customer's traction motor inverter with Lauterbach JTAG hardware, diagnosed and fixed DC bus instability, and built an STM32 proximity-sensing system end to end.

Raven is where it converges. It's a tilt-rotor eVTOL I designed and printed in carbon-fiber composite, the first airframe of a planned 4-drone autonomous swarm, and the flagship hardware of the stealth AI hardware startup I founded. FAA Part 107 certification is in progress.

groups.title: Embedded firmware & signal chains
items: End-to-end analog chains: sensor to ADC to IRQ to PWM/DAC to filter to amp

bullets: Own the EV electrical architecture for the 2027 car: BMS, thermal management, charging system, data acquisition, driver display, and all low-voltage electronics.
Building the charging cart from scratch: rebuilding the shutdown-circuit PCB, wiring, system architecture, CAD and 3D printing, and full documentation.
Powertrain electronics for an Emrax 228 motor with DTI motor controller on a ~120s4p Molicel P50B pack (~432 V nominal, 504 V max, ~8.6 kWh).
Exploring in-cell thermal-runaway sensing (DEP BRT) as an FSAE innovation concept; vendor validation cites roughly 12 minutes of pre-vent warning.
Debugged a customer's custom traction motor inverter using Lauterbach JTAG hardware and StellarStudio, resolving PWM timing faults, gate-driver issues, and current-sensing calibration errors.
Independently diagnosed DC bus instability and implemented a parallel-capacitor fix between the inverter and supply.
Built an STM32 proximity-sensing system end to end: ADC processing, timed interrupts, PWM/DAC audio output, analog low-pass filter, amplifier, speaker.
Authored PWM and space-vector modulation parameterization guides for internal and customer use; delivered competitive teardown analyses to ST marketing.

experience.role: Electrical Specialist, then High-Voltage & Co-Electrical Lead (BR1, 2026 car)
bullets: Programmed and configured the Orion BMS 2 for a 100s3p Molicel P45B accumulator (~360 V nominal, ~420 V max): cell monitoring, SoC algorithms, safety interlocks.
Programmed and installed a 540 V on-board charger and designed the charging cart with current/voltage control, safety shutdown, and deadman lock; verified FSAE EV compliance.
Designed schematic sections and reviewed PCBs for shutdown, charging-shutdown, precharge, discharge, and IMD circuits; caught protection-logic errors before fabrication.
Architected the vehicle CAN network, wrote STM32 firmware for sensor interfacing and diagnostics, and built a Python GUI for real-time CAN monitoring.
Soldered a 140+ thermistor accumulator harness; placement optimization improved measurement accuracy by roughly 50 percent (bench-validated).
Led team training on precision soldering, thermal architecture, and HV safety.
Managed Firebase and Firestore databases for an EdTech platform; debugged the iOS app through systematic testing.
Built company website infrastructure and implemented technical documentation.

experience.company: SCU Dept. of Mathematics & Computer Science
bullets: Tutored advanced mathematics and programming and graded coursework as assistant to Dr. Fady El Chidiac.
Conducted simulated penetration testing and observed real-time monitoring for critical infrastructure; worked in Linux kernel operations and embedded-systems security.
